chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.1022 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.5611/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.5612/2025 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application --------------------------------------------- 21.04.2025 Mr. Naveed Ahmed Narejo, advocate for applicant ------------------------------------------ Applicant/accused Muhammad Urs son of Ali Khan seeks protective bail in FIR No.399/2025, registered at P.S. Shah Latif Town, Karachi for offence under section 496-A, PPC. Learned counsel for applicant submits that applicant is innocent and he has been falsely implicated in this case for mala intentions and ulterior motives; that the alleged offence does not fall within the prohibitory clause of 497, Cr.PC; that he is a law abiding citizen and intends to surrender before the trial Court concerned but he apprehends his arrest at the hands of concerned police in order humiliate and disgrace him. Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicant/accused above named, for a period of five days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees twenty five thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicant is directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of five days from today or whenever the applicant surrenders before the trial Court, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicant fails to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, his bail bond shall stand automatically forfeited.

This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of five days from today or whenever the applicant surrenders before the trial Court, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicant fails to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, his bail bond shall stand automatically forfeited. The instant criminal bail application is accordingly disposed of. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
